qa_pairs.question: How do I mark new text for translation when making changes to an existing page?
qa_pairs.answer: You need to ensure that the new text to be translated is enclosed within <translate> </translate> tags.

qa_pairs.question: How should code blocks be handled when marking changes to a page for translation?
qa_pairs.answer: You should enclose code blocks in </translate> <translate> tags so that they are excluded from translation.

qa_pairs.question: What wiki content should be excluded from translation when marking page changes?
qa_pairs.answer: You should try to exclude code blocks and wiki markup, such as tables and tags, from translation.

qa_pairs.question: What are the steps to mark changes on a page for translation after editing?
qa_pairs.answer: First, go into 'View' mode. You should see the text 'This page has changes since it was last marked for translation.' at the top of the page; click on 'marked for translation'. Next, review the translation units to ensure code blocks and wiki markup are excluded and all plain text is included. Then, verify that 'fr' or 'en' appears as the priority language in the 'Priority languages' section. Finally, click on 'Mark this version for translation'.

qa_pairs.question: When is it appropriate to select the 'Do not invalidate translations' option on the 'Page translation' page?
qa_pairs.answer: You should only select this option if the change is minor, such as a typo, which wouldn't require the other-language version to be adjusted.

qa_pairs.question: Why are code blocks generally not translated by the professional human translator?
qa_pairs.answer: Our professional human translator is not a programmer and cannot distinguish between code and comments in every possible language, so the documentation team has instructed them to exclude code blocks from translation.

qa_pairs.question: What is the impact of code comments not being translated?
qa_pairs.answer: The value of explanatory comments within code blocks is decreased if they are not translated, which can affect the understanding of users reading the page in a different language.

qa_pairs.question: What are the suggested solutions for ensuring information in code block comments is translated?
qa_pairs.answer: You can move the information contained in the comments outside the code block into the surrounding text, leave an index comment (e.g., 'NOTE 1', 'NOTE 2') to connect external text to the code, or, if you're sufficiently bilingual and familiar with the translation apparatus, you may translate the code block yourself.

qa_pairs.question: What is the guideline regarding comments in example code for translation?
qa_pairs.answer: Please do not leave example code uncommented, but do remember that comments will not normally be translated, and consider how this will affect the user's understanding when reading the page in translation.

qa_pairs.question: How do I add a new item to the sidebar that needs to be translated?
qa_pairs.answer: First, add the new content to [[MediaWiki:Sidebar]], either as <tt>some-tag</tt> or, if it's a link, <tt>{{int:some-tag}}</tt>. Second, add the tags to [[MediaWiki:Sidebar-messages]]. Third, define the content of the tag in English on [[MediaWiki:some-tag]]. Lastly, translate the content of the tag on the specified translation page (https://docs.alliancecan.ca/mediawiki/index.php?title=Special:Translate&language=fr&group=wiki-sidebar&filter=!translated&action=translate).

qa_pairs.question: How are the tables on the 'Available software' page populated?
qa_pairs.answer: The tables on the 'Available software' page are automatically generated from module files in CVMFS.

qa_pairs.question: What is the process for adding a link to a new page from the 'Documentation' column on the 'Available software' tables?
qa_pairs.answer: To add a link, you need to add an entry to the `module_wiki_page.json` file, which is located at https://github.com/ComputeCanada/wiki_module_bot/blob/main/module_wiki_page.json. Ensure this change is added to the definitive copy of the file.

qa_pairs.question: How long does it take for changes made to the `module_wiki_page.json` file to be reflected on the 'Available software' page?
qa_pairs.answer: Changes may take up to six hours to propagate and appear on the page.
